sense.def: neg. prefix (a- before consonants; an- before vowels)
name of a Babylonian, father of Nidintu-Bel
name of a district in Armenia
name of the supreme god, Ahura Mazda
name of a Persian month, Nov.-Dec.
name of a Persian month, Oct.-Nov.
name of a Persian month, Dec.-Jan.
with ciy, Nom. sg. n. aniyašciy
to set in motion, go, come, arrive
Ariaramnes, name of the great-grandfather of Darius
name of one of the generals of Darius
name of one of the allies of Darius against Gaumāta
Arsames, name of the grandfather of Darius
I placed (my army) on floats of skins
name assumed by Martiya in his insurrection in Susiana
Otanes, name of one of the allies of Darius against Gaumata
name of the father of Aθrina
name of a fortress in Armenia
borne by oxen(?), mounted on camels
what is up from the earth
particle giving to preceding tuvam thou an indefinite force
name of a district in Media
name of a fortress in Arachosia
name assumed by Phraortes, the Median pretender
the Median pretender who assumed the name of Smerdis
name of a district in Arachosia
name of the father of Martiya
emphasizes slightly the word to which it is joined
Teispes, common ancestor of Cyrus and Darius (read cišpi or caišpi)
waning, growing old, as subs. completion, end
name of one of the commanders of Darius
name of a fortress in Armenia
with pointed cap, epithet of Scythians

entry.pos: conjunction; demonstrative pronoun; demonstrative pronoun used as a relative

sense.def: name of the father of Otanes
name of the father of Megabyzus
to hold 2 hold a position halt
to injure, deprive one (acc.) of something (acc.), take (acc.) from one (acc.)
name of a district in Babylonia
deceiving, false, a follower of the Druj
Nidintu-Bêl, name of a Babylonian rebel
Nebuchadrezar, the assumed name of the rebels Nidintu-Bêl and Arkha
Nabonidus, name of the last king of the new Babylonian empire
by name, written naamaa with fem. noun
on the other hand, moreover, again, once more
name of the father of Vidarna
Megabyzos, one of the allies of Darius in the defeat of false Smerdis
name of a Median tribe celebrated in priestcraft, Magian
month, with gen. of the name
appropriate, take to oneself, seize as one’s possession
name of the father of Intaphernes
much, to a great extent, in large numbers, utterly
name of the father of Ardumanish
all; with the gods of the royal house
and all things; and the royal residences; relating to the royal residences
Intaphernes, name of ally of Darius against the false Smerdis
Hydarnes, name of ally of Darius against the false Smerdis
name of a Persian month, Feb.-March
name of a fortress in Media
name of a region, according to Lassen, Sardis
army, hostile host, array of evil
designating a part of the Scythians in Dar. NRa. 3
of the race of Achaemenes, Achaemenidan
to hale to prison or inflict some form of penalty
name of the father of Arkha
